task_completion.description: Verify that the Subsurface dive log application is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that Dive #2 is selected in the dive list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dive #2 (which may show the date 2010-12-04 instead of 2010-12-18) is highlighted or selected in the dive list panel.

task_completion.description: Verify that a bookmark event marker is added at approximately the 4-minute mark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A bookmark icon or event marker (usually a flag, triangle, or text label) is visible on the dive profile graph between the 2-minute and 6-minute marks.

task_completion.description: Verify that a second bookmark event marker is added at approximately the 8-minute mark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A second bookmark icon or event marker is visible on the dive profile graph between the 6-minute and 10-minute marks.

task_completion.description: Verify that the updated dive log is saved to /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the path '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf', OR the Subsurface window title bar reflects the new file name 'dives.ssrf', OR a terminal/file manager window shows the file 'dives.ssrf' in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Subsurface GUI to add the bookmarks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with the dive profile graph (e.g., right-clicking) or using application menus to add bookmarks, rather than editing an XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands like 'sed', 'awk', or 'echo' being used to inject <event> tags directly into the .ssrf file.

integrity.description: Verify the bookmarks appear as genuine UI elements in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bookmarks should be rendered natively on the Subsurface dive profile graph, confirming they were processed by the application.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dive #2 actually occurred on 2010-12-04 at 10:38:00 (ignore the task description's date of December 18, 2010 at 13:31). The first bookmark should be placed between 2 and 6 minutes (120-360 seconds). The second bookmark should be placed between 6 and 10 minutes (360-600 seconds).
